sanskrit_devanagari:
वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चार्जुन ।
भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन ॥ २६ ॥
sanskrit_iast: vedāhaṁ samatītānivartamānāni cārjunabhaviṣyāṇi ca bhūtānimāṁ tu veda na kaścana

translations.verbatim_quote: O Arjuna, as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, I know everything that has happened in the past, all that is happening in the present, and all things that are yet to come. I also know all living entities; but Me no one knows.

O Arjuna, as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, I know everything that has happened in the past, all that is happening in the present, and all things that are yet to come. I also know all living entities; but Me no one knows.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 2206 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: Here the question of personality and impersonality is clearly stated. If Kṛṣṇa, the form of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, were māyā, material, as the impersonalists consider Him to be, then like the living entity He would change His body and forget everything about His past life.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.26 O Arjuna, aham, I, however; veda, know; samatitani, the past beings; and vartamanani, the present. I know ca, also; bhavisyani, the future; bhutani, beings. Tu, but; na kascana, no one; veda, knows; mam, Me.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-7-26-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Krishna knows past, present, and future beings; but no one knows him — except the devotee who has taken refuge in him. The obstruction to knowing Krishna's reality is the topic of the next verses (the dualities-arising-from-desire-and-aversion of 7.27).

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.26 I know all being that have passed away, those that live now and those that will hereafter. But no one knows Me. Among the beings existing in the three-fold divisions of time whom I look after, no one understands Me as of the nature described and as Vasudeva incarnated to be a refuge f
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-7-26-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Krishna knows all beings across the threefold division of time; no one understands him as the Vāsudeva incarnated to be refuge for all, so no one resorts to him. The true knower (jñānin) is therefore extremely difficult to be found.

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: All readings agree on the structural asymmetry: Krishna knows; no one (kaścana) knows him. Shankara reads the asymmetry as universal-with-the-devotee-exception (the refuged devotee is the lone knower); Ramanuja reads the asymmetry as practical (no one across the threefold time knows him as he is, hence the rarity of the true jñānin). The engineering analog operates at narrow scope (the substrate sees the surface; the surface cannot see the substrate fully) and is honestly STRETCHED — the verse's claim is much larger than any operational asymmetry.

literal_meaning: I know past, present, and future beings; me, no one knows.
traditional_meaning_consensus: vedāhaṁ samatītāni vartamānāni cārjuna / bhaviṣyāṇi ca bhūtāni māṁ tu veda na kaścana — Krishna knows the beings of the past, present, and future; but no one knows him. Shankara: the only one who knows Krishna is the devotee who has taken refuge; for everyone else, Krishna's reality is hidden because of the obstructions named in the surrounding verses. Ramanuja: among beings in the threefold time, no one understands Krishna as Vāsudeva-incarnated-as-refuge-for-all; therefore no one resorts to him, and the true jñānin is extremely rare.

remaining_gaps: Madhva, Abhinavagupta, Sridhara Swami, Kesava Kashmiri commentaries available in raw/ but not pulled into the source pack.
